heroHeadline: Pahalgam Attack First Anniversary: Modi Vows India Will 'Never Bow to Terror'; Black Marble Memorial Unveiled; India Opens Capitol Hill Terrorism Exhibition
heroSubtitle: One year after the Pahalgam massacre that killed 26 civilians and triggered Operation Sindoor, PM Narendra Modi declared on April 22, 2026 that 'India will never bow to any form of terror — the heinous designs of terrorists will never succeed.' A permanent black marble memorial bearing the names of all 26 victims was unveiled on the banks of the Lidder River in Pahalgam. Simultaneously, India's Ambassador Vinay Mohan Kwatra inaugurated the 'Human Cost of Terrorism' exhibition at US Capitol Hill, naming Pakistan-based organizations responsible for global attacks — a deliberate counter to Pakistan's rising international profile after its facilitation of the US-Iran ceasefire and Islamabad Talks.
footerNote: Data sourced from Indian MEA/MOD, Pakistan ISPR/MFA, UN Security Council, SIPRI, Crisis Group, HRW, Stimson Center
